judgeDetails.explanation: The predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as the gold standard.
Correctly identified exaggeration-minimisation and slogans, but missed flag-waving and misclassified loaded-language.
All predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as gold with exact matches.
Predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as gold.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard, with the same span and technique.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same span and technique as the gold standard.
Partial match. The predicted spans correctly identify the appeal-to-fear-prejudice technique but split the first gold standard span into two separate spans.
The predicted spans correctly identify the appeal-to-fear-prejudice technique but split the gold standard span into three separate spans.
Partial match: correct span but wrong technique for the first prediction, correct span but wrong technique for the second prediction.
The predicted spans correctly identify the bandwagon-reductio-ad-hitlerum technique but split the gold standard span into three separate spans.
The predicted spans correctly identify the same black-and-white fallacy as the gold standard, with equivalent technique detection.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard, with the exact same span and technique.
Correctly identified causal-oversimplification but misclassified black-and-white-fallacy for the second span.
One correct detection and one incorrect technique identified.
Partial match - correct technique detected but missed part of the manipulation.
One correct detection, one incorrect technique identified.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard.
Both predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as gold, with overlapping quotes and correct techniques.

The predicted output correctly identifies the flag-waving technique in the first two sentences and correctly identifies the slogan technique in the third sentence. However, it misses the fact that the first two sentences are part of a single manipulation as indicated in the gold standard.
Partial match. The predicted output correctly identified 'flag-waving' and 'slogans', but missed the 'appeal-to-fear-prejudice' technique in the first sentence.
Partial match. The predicted output correctly identified the first manipulation but also incorrectly identified the second span as loaded language.
The predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as the gold standard, with both sentences marked as loaded-language.
Both predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as the gold standard.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard.
Partial match: predicted spans are correct but missed the full repetition and incorrectly identified 'slogans'.
Predicted spans partially match gold standard. The repetition technique is correctly identified but the predicted span is shorter. The slogans technique is not present in the gold standard.
Correctly identified the slogan but incorrectly identified repetition.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard.
Partial match: correctly identified 'It is what it is' but over-detected other spans.
Partial match. The predicted spans correctly identify thought-terminating cliches but split them into three separate quotes, while the gold standard combines the first two into one span.
Predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as gold.
Partial match. The predicted spans correctly identify a straw man and appeal to fear, but the gold standard expects a combined whataboutism-straw-men-red-herring for the first part.
Predicted spans correctly identify the same manipulations as gold with exact technique matches.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard.

The predicted output correctly identifies the reductio-ad-hitlerum technique but misses the bandwagon aspect. It also misclassifies the second part as loaded-language instead of bandwagon-reductio-ad-hitlerum.
The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard, with the same span and technique.
Correctly identified causal-oversimplification but incorrectly identified loaded-language.
Correctly identified doubt, but misclassified appeal-to-popularity as appeal-to-authority and thought-terminating-cliches.

The predicted output correctly identifies the same manipulation as the gold standard with the same technique.

Correct technique for one detection, but missed the repetition technique in the second detection.
The predicted output correctly identifies the thought-terminating cliches but splits them into separate quotes, which is not as precise as the gold standard.
The predicted spans correctly identify the techniques but split the sentence into two parts, which is not as precise as the gold standard.
